@graph.rdfs:label: The Exodian Church
@graph.sa:description: A quasi-religious, tech-salvational movement that preaches the fusion of faith and technology as the path to transcendence. The Church operates across all hive cities, recruiting members from every social stratum with an apocalyptic vision: the old universe is ending, and through sacred engineering and spiritual refinement, the worthy ascend. They see Manfuctors as prophets, technology as scripture, and the space elevators as humanity's path to salvation. Most established institutions (House Valorian, the Synthesis, city governments) view them as heretical anarchists; the Church views the establishments as corrupt guardians of obsolete power. They are growing, decentralized, and increasingly active. Their high priests and senior liturgists are unmistakable on sight: fully augmented lower bodies mounted in armored reliquary frames with four shield-like legs, turning ritual procession into mobile doctrine. Those chassis are both vestment and warning, an icon of a Church that will not be cowed.

sa:role.rdfs:label: Novitiate
sa:role.sa:prerequisites: Accepted into Church via initiation ritual; pledged to share faith and witness the sacred fusion of hand and spirit.
sa:description: Membership in local Church congregation; access to meditation halls and communal spaces
Spiritual guidance from ordained Exodians; instruction in sacred tech-philosophy
Access to shared resources: food, shelter, healing for Novitiates in need (mutual aid)
Right to participate in Exodian community work projects (shrine building, tech salvage expeditions)
Recruitment authority: can invite others to the Church; earn spiritual merit for each convert

sa:role.rdfs:label: Ordained Exodian
sa:role.sa:prerequisites: Novitiate for two years, deep study of Exodian scripture, passed theological examination, initiated into higher mysteries.
sa:description: Authority to lead congregation worship and administer sacraments (blessings of tech, absolution for doubt)
Teach new Novitiates; grow local Church presence through proselytizing
Access to restricted Exodian grimoires and technical schematics of sacred artifacts
Stipend: 300 credits monthly from Church tithes and donations
Authority to request 1d6 Novitiates for Church work (shrine building, equipment repair, salvage expeditions)
Vow of poverty: personal property renounced, but Church provides all material needs

sa:role.rdfs:label: Tech Evangelist
sa:role.sa:prerequisites: Proven ability to locate, recover, and sanctify abandoned or hoarded technology; accepted into the Church's field ministry.
sa:description: Lead salvage sermons, recovery runs, and public demonstrations that frame lost tech as sacred inheritance
Free seized technology from corrupt owners, black markets, or derelict ruins and redistribute it to the faithful
Access to Church recovery teams, basic relic appraisal, and sanctification rites for reclaimed machines
Permission to recruit scavengers, hackers, and tinkerers into Exodian service
Small stipend and priority claim on reclaimed tools, components, and repair access

sa:role.rdfs:label: High Priest of the Reliquary
sa:role.sa:prerequisites: Served as Ordained Exodian or Tech Evangelist for several years; mastered Exodian liturgy, recovery doctrine, and reliquary maintenance; chosen by senior clergy.
sa:description: Command the Church's ritual salvage operations; decide which relics are freed, consecrated, or dismantled for sacred use
Wear the full augmented reliquary chassis seen in procession: armored lower body, shield-legs, and sacred tech-vault interfaces
Lead major rites, public marches, and reclamation ceremonies that turn recovery into a visible declaration of faith
Direct squads of Tech Evangelists and Ordained Exodians during high-risk tech extraction missions
Access to sealed reliquary stores, advanced augmentation maintenance, and the Church's most guarded recovery intelligence

sa:role.rdfs:label: Circle Speaker
sa:role.sa:prerequisites: High Priest of the Reliquary or equivalent senior office for several years; demonstrated sector-wide influence, successful recovery of significant lost technology, and proven ability to unite multiple congregations.
sa:description: Authority over multiple congregations across one or two hive cities; strategic religious direction and recovery doctrine
Coordinate recruitment across city boundaries and direct large-scale reclamation campaigns
Command authority: direct 2d6 Ordained Exodians, 2d4 Tech Evangelists, and 2d4 Novitiates in organized activities
Access to Church's hidden technology vault; retrieve pre-Exodian relics for research, restoration, or redistribution
Discretionary budget: 20,000 credits annually for expansion, recovery drives, and sacred construction
Right to confiscate or liberate tech from non-believers if deemed a holy recovery
Organizational authority: call for coordinated prayer vigils, protests, sit-ins, or recovery raids across cities

sa:role.rdfs:label: Cardinal of the Liberated Forge
sa:role.sa:prerequisites: Served as Circle Speaker for six years, orchestrated Church growth across three+ hive cities, and proven ability to liberate major technology caches or industrial sites under Church control.
sa:description: Seat on Exodian High Synod; strategic input on Church direction, recovery doctrine, and long-term apocalyptic plans
Authority over all Church operations in assigned sector (typically 2–3 hive cities)
Command 4d6 Circle Speakers, multiple subordinate Ordained Exodians, and 2d4 elite recovery cadres
Direct access to Church leader (the Prophet, whose identity is secret even to most Cardinals)
Discretionary budget: 100,000+ credits annually for organized operations
Authority to authorize holy work: recovery operations, technology redistribution, and sanctioned seizures from corrupt powers
Ability to move Church resources and personnel across city boundaries with minimal detection
Build personal fanatical following; orders are treated as divine mandate by subordinates
